decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a Bayesian phylogeographic generalized linear model to reconstruct HIV-1C spatial spread and assess epidemiological predictors and sampling bias.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the spatiotemporal diffusion of HIV-1 subtype C in Brazil.
supported_label_variants: Bayesian phylogeographic generalized linear model approach

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates the geographic origin and timing of an HIV-1 subtype F outbreak from viral sequences using phylogenetic and Bayesian inference.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic reconstruction of an HIV outbreak spanning Spain and Western Europe.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Explicitly uses mitochondrial markers and phylogeographic methods to compare the geographic population structure of two Lyme disease reservoir hosts.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Disease-relevant application examining reservoir expansion and implications for Lyme disease spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The abstract describes a phylogeographic pattern as background and reports an ancient H. pylori genome, but does not explicitly state that a phylogeographic method was applied.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is neither an explicit methodological source nor a clearly documented application of the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogenetic, phylodynamic, and phylogeographical reconstruction to trace the origins and introduction routes of HIV-1 subtype A1 into Italy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to HIV lineage diffusion between Africa, Eastern Europe, and Italy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylogeographic analysis to whole SFTSV genomes to infer the origin and overseas transmission of genotype B.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application to SFTSV transmission among South Korea, Japan, and China.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Investigates Sporothrix phylogeography using multilocus genetic data and geographically restricted subclusters to interpret dispersal patterns.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to pathogenic fungal lineages associated with human and animal infections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylogeographical analysis to estimate the African origin, divergence times, and geographic dispersal of HPV-16 lineages.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the ancient geographic diffusion of a human virus.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylogeographic analysis to identify the common source and global transmission pathways of deformed wing virus.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to global pathogen spread in honeybees.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole genomes and temporally structured Bayesian sequence analysis to reconstruct spatial phylogenetic trees and international transmission of Salmonella Typhimurium DT104.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic reconstruction of the emergence and global diffusion of a bacterial pathogen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines a global Mycobacterium tuberculosis lineage phylogeny with geographic mapping to infer origins and migration-driven dispersal.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pathogen phylogeography of the LAM tuberculosis lineage.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts a phylogeographical study of globally sampled brown dog ticks and relates phylogenetic lineages to geography and climate.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Disease-vector application with implications for geographically varying pathogen transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper summarizes historical conclusions from phylodynamic and phylogeographic studies but does not explicitly apply, develop, diagnose, or directly critique the exact method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Its methodological discussion is broad and does not establish it as a lineage source for phylogeography itself.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts phylogeographic analyses of HIV recombinant-form sequences to reconstruct early origins and migration routes across West and Central Africa.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the dissemination of HIV-1 CRF02_AG and CRF06_cpx.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole viral genomes and a continuous phylogeographic diffusion approach to reconstruct the origin and spread of Argentina's foot-and-mouth disease epidemic.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic diffusion analysis of a livestock epidemic.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses mitochondrial sequences and geographic population sampling to resolve phylogeographical structure in a whitefly vector across South and Southeast Asia.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Vector phylogeography is applied to understand begomovirus spread and control.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole-genome sequences obtained by multiplex PCR-based sequencing for phylogeographic analysis linking human hantavirus cases to rodent strains in space and time.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application to source tracing of hantavirus infections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogeographic analyses to infer the timing and routes of multiple DENV-1 lineage introductions into Goiás.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to dengue-virus dispersion during an outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies Bayesian phylogeographic analyses to influenza sequences to reconstruct introductions, regional exchange, and local lineage persistence in South America.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to influenza dissemination dynamics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes complete Marburg virus genomes in a Bayesian phylogeographical framework to infer ancestral locations and migration rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the origin and geographic dispersion of Marburg virus.

decisions.relationship_evidence: This is a broad review of HIV molecular epidemiology applications that mentions phylogeographic analyses but does not itself apply or specifically evaluate the exact method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The discussion covers several molecular-epidemiology approaches and is not explicit guidance for phylogeography alone.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses geographically diverse microsatellite data to conduct phylogeographic analysis and associate Blastomyces populations with freshwater drainage basins.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the geographic population structure of fungal pathogens.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses geographically separated human and macaque samples, haplotype networks, and sequence analysis to investigate Plasmodium knowlesi phylogeography.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application distinguishing two geographically separated zoonotic parasite types.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts a phylogeographic study of H5N1 sequences using a Bayesian framework and continuous-time Markov-chain diffusion model to reconstruct spread along a flyway.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to host-associated avian influenza migration.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogeographical analysis to estimate the timing and sources of HCV and HIV introductions among former blood donors in China.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to blood-borne virus transmission history.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares the geographic genetic structures of an anther-smut pathogen and its host to demonstrate strong phylogeographic co-structure.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete comparative application to host-pathogen geographic structure and dispersal.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes long mitochondrial sequences from European Echinococcus isolates to resolve phylogeography and compare geographic resolution with shorter sequences.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to a zoonotic tapeworm genotype infecting humans and livestock.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses 331 globally sampled Shigella dysenteriae genomes to reconstruct historical worldwide spread and geographically associated lineages.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete whole-genome phylogeographic reconstruction of an epidemic bacterial pathogen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies a Bayesian phylogeographic approach to genome-scale H7N7 data to reconstruct supported migration events and diffusion patterns.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to global influenza-virus spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines viral gene sequences with epidemiological information to infer the geographic spread patterns of IHNV and VHSV among Italian trout farms.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete molecular phylogeographic application to two aquaculture viruses.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses viral sequences for phylogeography to estimate the origin, westward spread, and regional transmission chains of BVDV-1f.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to livestock-virus spread in Northern Italy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines Bayesian phylogeography with landscape-resistance models to reconstruct and prospectively project vampire-bat rabies invasion routes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to rabies spatial dynamics and invasion forecasting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses long mitochondrial sequences to evaluate the genetic variability and phylogeography of zoonotic Echinococcus genotype G1 across the Americas.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to a globally important zoonotic parasite.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Examines comparative phylogeography and gene flow in African fruit bats to assess plausible reservoir-mediated Ebola virus dispersal.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Disease-relevant application to potential filovirus reservoir movement between Central and West Africa.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogenetic and Bayesian coalescent analyses to study the origin and phylogeographic patterns of equine influenza outbreaks in South America.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the spatiotemporal spread of an equine virus.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses sequence-based Bayesian methods to reconstruct the spatiotemporal spread and biogeographic history of HCV across Pakistan.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to HCV origin and geographic spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies discrete phylogeographic analysis to whole-genome rabies data to identify long-distance dispersal and fine-scale lineage circulation in Tanzania.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application to endemic rabies transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces a relaxed directional random walk model for continuous-trait evolution, supplies efficient likelihood computation and Bayesian inference, and demonstrates improved viral dispersal reconstruction.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The paper explicitly introduces and implements a new diffusion model applicable to continuous phylogeography.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian coalescent analysis and phylogeographic reconstruction to infer the origin and dissemination of HIV-1 complex recombinant forms in Africa.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to HIV lineage migration and epidemic history.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole-genome surveillance data and spatial phylogenetic analysis to distinguish local persistence from reintroduction of H7N9 between epidemic waves.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application to avian influenza persistence and dissemination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian inference software to construct phylogeographic and phylodynamic profiles and infer HIV migration routes in southern Spain.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to regional HIV epidemic diversification and movement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses globally sampled Toxoplasma sequences, timed phylogeny, and geostatistical analyses to infer origin and reconstruct worldwide spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic application to a globally distributed protozoan pathogen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Tests a lyssavirus origin hypothesis using probabilistic phylogeography and ancestral-distribution reconstruction from gene and complete-genome sequences.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the global origin and transmission history of lyssaviruses.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies an extended multilocus sequence scheme to a global Brucella collection to infer geographic lineage origins and worldwide spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the phylogeography of a zoonotic bacterial genus.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogeographic approaches to reconstruct the historical movement and host transitions of Rift Valley fever virus.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to viral movement across Africa and the Arabian Peninsula.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses detailed spatial genetic characterization of rodent hosts and their arenaviruses to assess how host phylogeographic structure constrains virus spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete host-pathogen application explaining the geographic restriction of arenaviruses.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs a Bayesian phylogeographic study and fits discrete-trait generalized linear models to identify predictors of avian influenza diffusion in China.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to regional viral transmission routes and spatial diffusion determinants.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses genomic comparisons to identify phylogeographic structure between carbapenem-resistant Enterobacteriaceae sampled on opposite US coasts.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application to transmission structure in antimicrobial-resistant pathogens.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole-genome phylogenetic and phylogeographic analysis to reconstruct local and international seeding of Legionella populations in hospitals.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application to nosocomial pathogen introduction and source investigation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares a complete PPRV genome with global sequences to estimate divergence and explore geographic ancestry and lineage relationships.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application to the evolutionary geography of a transboundary livestock virus.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylodynamic tools for an in-depth phylogeographic analysis of HCV subtype 1a migration into and within Italy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to HCV migration patterns relevant to sequencing policy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Characterizes H5N8 isolates from poultry and wild birds by geographic and evolutionary grouping to infer persistence and reintroduction sources in South Korea.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographical characterization of an avian influenza outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses geolocated dengue genotype and serotype data to quantify local transmission-chain structure and viral mixing across spatial scales.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete fine-scale spatial-genetic application to endemic dengue transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Explicitly uses a phylogeographic approach with pathogen sequence data to infer Lasiodiplodia distribution, diversity, and possible entry routes into Brazil.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the geographic spread of a plant pathogen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines newly generated Zika virus genomes with ecological, geographic, and epidemiological data to estimate establishment and international dissemination before detection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic reconstruction of Zika virus diffusion in Brazil and the Americas.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses partial viral-genome sequencing and host barcoding across Canada to resolve geographic rabies-virus variants, host jumps, and lineage emergence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pathogen phylogeography across bat reservoirs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylogeographic and phylodynamic models to reconstruct influenza lineage seeding, migration, and seasonal transmission in Brazil.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to influenza diffusion during pandemic and post-pandemic periods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reconstructs virus and satellite-RNA phylogenies and uses them to infer their phylogeography across Asia.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the geographic evolution and movement of a plant virus and its satellite RNA.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies analytical methods based on phylogeographic reconstruction to compare rabies-virus diffusivity and environmental drivers across hosts and landscapes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete comparative application to spatial dynamics of multiple rabies epidemics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses time-resolved viral phylogenies and geographic data to reconstruct global, regional, and local CV-A16 migration pathways.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to enterovirus transmission across multiple geographic scales.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents an exact numerical structured-coalescent solution and an improved approximation after identifying bias and computational limitations in existing phylogeographic methods.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development, with the new methods implemented and compared under multiple scenarios.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies discrete and continuous-space phylogeographical models to complete West Nile virus genomes to reconstruct entry and spread in Europe and Italy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application to West Nile virus dispersion.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper develops article-mining methods for obtaining geographic metadata used by phylogeographic studies, rather than applying or implementing phylogeographic inference itself.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an ancillary metadata-extraction contribution, not a lineage source for the exact analytical concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylogeographic approaches to 289 whole rabies-virus genomes to identify local cross-border leakage and long-distance incursions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete whole-genome application to transboundary rabies outbreaks.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses complete mitochondrial gene sequences, phylogenetic trees, and geographic sampling to infer Angiostrongylus structure, gene flow, and likely origins.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic application to a zoonotic parasite.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole genomes, SNP and VNTR genotyping, and fine-scale maps to examine temporal phylogeographic patterns of Yersinia pestis in Madagascar.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application to the long-term maintenance and dispersal of plague.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses global viral gene sequences and Bayesian inference to investigate IHNV phylogeography, ancestry, and spatial differentiation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the circulation of an aquaculture pathogen, with emphasis on France.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylogeographic modelling of HBV/B5 genomes to infer ancestral locations and westward spread across the Arctic.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to hepatitis B virus and host co-expansion.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses global whole-genome datasets and longitudinal phylogeographic data to propose the origin and migration history of an emerging tuberculosis lineage.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application to an emerging multidrug-resistant Mycobacterium tuberculosis clone.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylogeographic analysis to dengue patient samples to identify two co-circulating lineages during the 2016 Buenos Aires outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete outbreak application to dengue-virus circulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses coalescent-based phylogeographic inference to investigate norovirus migration patterns, origins, and multicenter spread in China.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to national norovirus diffusion.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits continuous and discrete phylogeographic models to georeferenced bluetongue-virus genomes to estimate environmental and host-related diffusion drivers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to landscape-scale viral movement in Europe.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogenetic and Bayesian analyses to infer the geographic origins and introductions of HIV-1 non-B variants in southern Spain.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic application to immigration-associated HIV lineage movement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines parasite sequences from Panama with global data and haplotype networks to evaluate phylogeography, connectivity, and long-range dispersal.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to geographically widespread molluscan parasites.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses 134 globally sampled Yersinia pseudotuberculosis genomes to identify an Asian ancestry and subsequent dispersal of clonal lineages.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete population-genomic phylogeography of a human intestinal pathogen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines epidemiological tracing with MERS-CoV genomes to create a phylogeographic tree and examine the geospatial timeline of viral evolution.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application to the global spread and zoonotic introductions of MERS.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses molecular data and phylogeographic analysis to characterize host-associated Trypanosoma genotypes and transmission patterns in Colombian livestock.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to livestock trypanosome transmission dynamics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylogeographic analysis to BVDV sequence datasets to reconstruct origins, spatiotemporal distributions, and viral flows relevant to control.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to prioritizing livestock-virus control areas in Italy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogeographic analysis of HIV sequences to reconstruct virus movement within Ukraine before and during war.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application linking HIV dissemination to conflict-related displacement and behavior.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylogeography to estimate ancestral hosts and cross-species transitions of variola virus and other orthopoxviruses.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to viral evolutionary history and host transitions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies Bayesian phylogeographic analysis to influenza sequences to predict the sources and spatiotemporal migration of Taiwanese epidemics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to influenza outbreak origins and international movement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylogeographic models to reconstruct the origin and dispersion routes of HIV-1 CRF31_BC in Brazil.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to national HIV recombinant-form diffusion.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses 433 complete EEEV genomes and phylogeographic analysis to test and support a source-sink transmission model across US states.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application to mosquito-borne virus transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses near-complete mitochondrial sequences and Bayesian phylogeographic analysis to identify supported diffusion routes and a probable origin for Echinococcus genotype G3.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to an epidemiologically important zoonotic parasite genotype.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses novel Bayesian phylogeography methods and viral sequences to reconstruct H5N1 transmission routes and identify factors associated with spread in Indonesia.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to avian influenza transmission and control planning.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies Bayesian phylogenetics and phylogeography to complete Brucella genomes to infer geographic origins and global distribution pathways.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete whole-genome application to a zoonotic bacterial pathogen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study explicitly presents a phylogeographic analysis of geographically sampled PRRSV-1 sequences to characterize viral distribution across Guangdong.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the geographic circulation of a major swine pathogen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses range-wide tick and endosymbiont sequences to compare geographic phylogenetic patterns and demonstrate concordant population divisions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Disease-vector application with implications for dispersal and associated vector-borne diseases.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses mitochondrial sequences and geographically sampled cattle ticks to infer lineage distribution, migration, and range expansion across African regions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Vector phylogeography applied to a tick central to East Coast fever transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses mitochondrial phylogenetics and population-genetic sampling to characterize snail phylogeography, invasion history, and geographic parasite associations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete host-parasite application involving an intermediate host of digenetic trematodes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates how random sequence-subsampling schemes affect root-location accuracy and predictor-signal suppression in Bayesian phylogeographic generalized linear models.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance and bias assessment of Bayesian virus phylogeography.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts phylogeographic ancestral-state reconstruction by parsimony on full HBV genomes to compare the global dissemination of genotypes A and D.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to hepatitis B virus origins and geographic clustering.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates the representativeness and transmission estimates of whole-genome versus multilocus discrete-trait phylogeography models for variola virus.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit empirical assessment of phylogeographic model performance under different genomic inputs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses genome-wide SNPs in discrete and continuous phylogeographic analyses to resolve fine-scale Salmonella Napoli diffusion and probable origin.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete high-resolution genomic application to human salmonellosis transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Demonstrates that an earlier landscape-phylogeographic association was a false positive caused by missing negative controls and compares discrete and continuous approaches.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit diagnosis of inferential failure and guidance on controls for viral landscape phylogeography.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs a comparative-genomics phylogeographic investigation of 53 Brucella canis strains to infer lineages and a possible South American spread route.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete whole-genome application to a zoonotic bacterial pathogen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses phylogeographic analyses, molecular networks, and a Slatkin-Maddison approach to identify HIV transmission hotspots and viral gene flow.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to local HIV geospatial diffusion in Germany.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a Bayesian phylogeography framework to reconstruct the origin and spatial dissemination of HBV genotype C in East Asia.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to hepatitis B virus epidemic waves and geographic diffusion.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses geographically sampled parasite sequences, maximum-likelihood trees, and haplotype networks to assess phylogeographic structure across Asian regions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete molecular phylogeographic application to the principal cause of human angiostrongyliasis and a sibling species.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylogenetic and phylogeographic analyses of potato virus S sequences to infer its origin and interregional migration pathways.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the historical global diffusion of a plant pathogen.
